city: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
casualties_reported: Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
casualties_source: Kuwaiti MoD / Italian Foreign Ministry / Stars and Stripes
notes: 386th Air Expeditionary Wing hub. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted initial ballistic missiles on Feb 28; debris fell on base. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to 12+ structures, aircraft shelters, and runway areas. Camp Canada (Canadian forces) bunkers also damaged. On March 2, Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15Es in friendly fire incident — all 6 crew ejected safely. Italian 51st Stormo personnel sheltered in bunkers.

casualties_reported: Two missiles reached base on March 1. Early warning radar targeted. No US casualties reported.
casualties_source: Qatar Defence Ministry / QNA / Al Jazeera
notes: CENTCOM forward HQ. Qatar Defence Ministry said it thwarted attacks per pre-approved security plan. Two missiles reached base on March 1 per Qatar's official news agency. Long-range early warning radar in northern Qatar also targeted. Dozens of explosions heard in Doha from Patriot intercepts.

casualties_reported: 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
casualties_source: UAE MoD / Abu Dhabi Media Office / Xinhua / CNBC
notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

casualties_reported: 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
casualties_source: Bahrain Ministry of Interior / Bahrain Defence Force / Xinhua
notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

casualties_reported: 1 French chief warrant officer KIA. Several French soldiers wounded. 1 airport guard killed. Iraqi Kurdish casualties reported. 27 total killed in Iraq (including 21 pro-Iran faction fighters, 3 Iranian Kurdish fighters, 1 civilian near Baghdad).
casualties_source: KRG / ACLED / French MoD / Al Jazeera
notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): 6 Kurdish Peshmerga killed, 30 wounded in Iranian ballistic missile strikes north of Erbil. Cumulative: US consulate area, airport targeted. French soldier KIA. Kurdish Peshmerga positions targeted.

casualties_reported: Minor injuries
notes: Repeat target from 2020 Iranian retaliation. US forces dispersed after lessons learned. [NOTE: Limited specific sourcing for Ain al-Asad during 2026 conflict. Entry based on pattern of attacks on US facilities across Iraq and confirmed IRGC claims of striking 27+ bases. Specific active_days and casualty details require further verification.]

weapon: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified)
casualties_reported: Casualties reported but Haifa-specific figures unconfirmed. Major Israeli casualties were in Beit Shemesh (9 killed) and Tel Aviv, not Haifa.
notes: Hezbollah targeted missile defense site south of Haifa on March 2, first strikes since 2024 ceasefire. Hezbollah also launched drone swarm at Ramat David airbase targeting radar sites and control rooms. Direct Iranian missile fire at Haifa unverified — most Haifa-area attacks attributed to Hezbollah.

weapon: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
casualties_reported: 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
casualties_source: IDF / Magen David Adom / Israeli media
notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

casualties_reported: Runway damage, aircraft losses not confirmed
notes: Home of Israeli F-35I Adir squadron. Priority target for Iran. [NOTE: Nevatim was confirmed targeted in 2024 and 2025 Iran-Israel exchanges. Targeting during 2026 conflict is plausible but specific damage reports for this war unverified in open-source reporting as of March 13.]

casualties_reported: All intercepted — no impact
notes: Symbolic targeting near Israeli nuclear facility. Arrow-3 exoatmospheric intercepts. [NOTE: Symbolic targeting plausible given precedent. Arrow-3 intercepts plausible. But specific confirmed reporting of Dimona-area strikes during 2026 war not found in open sources as of March 13.]

weapon: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
casualties_reported: 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
casualties_source: Lebanese Health Ministry / OCHA / ACLED / CIR
notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

casualties_reported: Included in Lebanese total
notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

casualties_reported: Infrastructure damage — no casualties reported
notes: World's largest oil loading facility. Saudi Arabia confirmed shooting down two drones, debris started limited fire. No civilian injuries. Saudi Aramco warned of 'catastrophic consequences' for oil markets. Saudi Arabia increasingly diverted oil to Red Sea port of Yanbu via East-West Crude Oil Pipeline.

weapon: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
casualties_reported: 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
casualties_source: UKMTO / Bloomberg / Precious Shipping / IRGC claims / Windward / CRS
notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

city: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
casualties_reported: 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
notes: 378th Air Expeditionary Wing. 2,300+ US troops stationed. Chinese satellite imagery showed 13 KC-135 tankers, 6 E-3 Sentries, 4 E-11 BACN aircraft pre-war. Saudi MoD confirmed intercepting multiple missiles. Iranian state media claimed hits on command centers and fuel storage (unconfirmed by CENTCOM). Sgt. Pennington was 7th US combat death of conflict. Pennington assigned to 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade.

casualties_reported: 9 killed (including 3 teenage siblings), 49 wounded, 11 missing
casualties_source: Magen David Adom / IDF / Israeli media
notes: Deadliest single strike on Israeli civilians in the conflict. Iranian ballistic missile struck synagogue and public bomb shelter in residential neighborhood, 35km west of Jerusalem. Roof of reinforced shelter collapsed. Two interceptors had been launched against the missile but failed. Demonstrated limits of Israeli missile defense even for reinforced shelters. Three Bitton siblings (ages 16, 15, 13) among the dead. United Hatzalah volunteer Ronit Elimelech and her mother Sarah also killed.

casualties_reported: 6 US Army Reserve soldiers KIA, multiple wounded
casualties_source: DoD / CENTCOM / CNN / Stars and Stripes
notes: Deadliest single attack on US forces in the conflict. Direct hit on makeshift tactical operations center (triple-wide trailer) at civilian port, ~9am local time March 1. One projectile penetrated air defenses. All 6 KIA were from 103rd Sustainment Command (Army Reserve, Des Moines, Iowa): Capt. Cody Khork (35), SFC Nicole Amor (39), SFC Noah Tietjens (42), Sgt. Declan Coady (20), Maj. Jeffrey O'Brien, CWO3 Robert Marzan. Bodies of two initially unaccounted for, recovered from burning building. Dignified transfer at Dover AFB attended by President Trump on March 8.

casualties_reported: 1 US National Guard soldier died of health-related incident on March 6 (Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, NYPD officer). Not classified as combat death.
notes: Northeastern Kuwait. Iranian drone struck the US garrison. Maj. Davius died during medical emergency at Camp Buehring on March 6 — incident under investigation, classified as health-related, not combat. IRGC claimed 230-drone wave targeting Camp Buehring among other Kuwait facilities on Day 6.

casualties_reported: 14 people injured
notes: Jordan intercepted 2 ballistic missiles on Feb 28. Total of 119 Iranian missiles and drones targeted Jordan. 14 people injured. Jordan has historically cooperated with US/Israeli air defense operations.

casualties_reported: No casualties reported
notes: British air force base on Mediterranean island of Cyprus struck by Iranian drone. No casualties. Represents furthest west Iranian retaliation reached. UK sovereign base area.

casualties_reported: At least 1 fuel storage tank damaged in Duqm
notes: Oman's deep-water ports (Duqm, Salalah, Sohar) are potential bypass routes around Strait of Hormuz closure. Drone strikes on Duqm and Salalah damaged fuel infrastructure and discouraged alternative routing. Sohar fell within insurer war risk area. Oman was only GCC country Iran did not strike on Day 1, given its mediator role. These strikes represent escalation against a traditional neutral party. First_strike date approximate — verify against maritime intelligence dailies.

casualties_reported: Minor injuries to airport workers. Limited material damage to passenger terminal. 1 girl later died from shrapnel injuries (March 4). Radar equipment damaged March 16.
casualties_source: Kuwaiti General Authority of Civil Aviation / Kuwaiti Ministry of Health / Reuters
notes: Civilian airport struck by drone on evening of Feb 28. Kuwaiti authorities confirmed minor injuries and damage to passenger building. On March 4, Kuwaiti Ministry of Health reported death of a girl from shrapnel-induced injuries — likely related to airport or broader Kuwait strikes. On March 16, radar equipment damaged by Iranian strike.

casualties_reported: Unknown — embassy personnel sheltering in place
notes: US Embassy in Kuwait likely hit by Iranian missile strike per Wikipedia citing multiple sources. Xinhua published screenshot showing smoke billowing near embassy area on March 2. US Embassy warned public not to approach, all personnel sheltering in place. US embassies in Saudi Arabia and UAE consulate in Dubai also reportedly targeted by drones during the week.

weapon: N/A — aircraft loss not due to hostile or friendly fire
casualties_reported: 6 US crew members killed
notes: US KC-135 refueling aircraft lost over western Iraq on March 12. All 6 crew confirmed dead by March 13. CENTCOM confirmed loss was NOT due to hostile fire or friendly fire. Under investigation. Brings total US military deaths in conflict to 13. KC-135s from Prince Sultan Air Base form backbone of strike operations — loss highlights vulnerability of tanker fleet.

casualties_reported: No casualties reported. Oil-loading operations suspended twice.
notes: Key strategic target — Fujairah is the terminus of the UAE's Habshan-Fujairah pipeline (1.5 mbd capacity), the primary bypass route for oil exports around the Strait of Hormuz. First attack March 14: intercepted drone debris struck bunkering facility, starting fire and suspending oil-loading. Second attack March 16: direct drone strike, oil-loading suspended again. Two shutdowns in three days signals deliberate Iranian strategy to close the Hormuz bypass. If Fujairah stays offline, the 12.35 mbd shortfall widens to 13.85 mbd.

casualties_reported: No casualties reported. All flights suspended for several hours.
notes: World's busiest airport for international passengers struck by Iranian drone hitting a fuel tank on March 16. All flights suspended for several hours. Emirates diverted flights to Abu Dhabi and Al Maktoum Airport; one Dublin-bound flight turned around over Egypt. Emirates resumed limited service after 10am local. Represents significant escalation — targeting the UAE's primary civilian aviation hub and a symbol of Gulf prosperity. Hundreds of thousands of travelers affected.

casualties_reported: No casualties reported. Fire started.
notes: Fourth attack on Fujairah Oil Industry Zone since March 9. Drone strike sparked fires. Part of deliberate Iranian strategy to close the Hormuz bypass route via Fujairah. The Habshan-Fujairah (ADCOP) pipeline (1.5 mbd capacity) runs to Fujairah — repeated attacks have suspended oil loadings multiple times. Bunkering volumes down 60%+.

city: Shah Oil Field / Al Bahyan, Abu Dhabi
casualties_reported: 1 Palestinian civilian killed in Al Bahyan by missile hitting civilian car. Fire at Shah oil field.
notes: Drone strike ignited fire at Shah oil field. Separately, a missile hit a civilian car in Al Bahyan district, killing a Palestinian national. Ruwais Industrial Complex (Abu Dhabi's largest refinery, 922,000 bpd) remains shut from earlier attacks. Cumulative UAE attacks: 314 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,672 drones since Feb 28.

casualties_reported: Fire in residential building — casualties unknown.
notes: Drone attack caused fire in a residential building in Umm Al Quwain. Part of broader March 17 Gulf attack wave.

casualties_reported: Minor structural damage, no casualties
notes: Kuwait-flagged LPG tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah struck by projectile 23 nautical miles east of Fujairah at 23:01 UTC March 17. This was the 17th attack on commercial vessels since February 28.

casualties_reported: No casualties confirmed. Sirens triggered.
notes: Explosive drones targeted the US Embassy compound in Baghdad, triggering sirens. Explosion heard near the diplomatic compound.

origin: Iran (IRGC — '57th wave of Operation True Promise 4')
casualties_reported: 2 elderly civilians killed (couple in their 70s, Ramat Gan). 1 wounded (25-year-old, shrapnel, Bnei Brak). Tel Aviv train station damaged.
casualties_source: NPR / Times of Israel / PressTV / IDF
notes: IRGC launched combined drone and ballistic missile attacks on central Israel in retaliation for Larijani killing. Cluster-munition warheads from ballistic missile killed couple in Ramat Gan. ~50% of ballistic missiles now carry cluster warheads to bypass Israeli defenses. Cumulative Israeli casualties: 16+ killed, 3,400+ wounded. Iran claims 'over 100 military and security targets' hit. 290 of 410-440 Iranian launch vehicles destroyed — daily missile volume down from ~100 on Day 1 to single digits by Day 18.

casualties_reported: No Australian personnel injured per PM Albanese.
notes: A projectile fell near an Australian airbase in the UAE. PM Albanese confirmed no personnel injured.

casualties_reported: Extensive damage. LNG Trains 4 and 6 damaged (12.8 MTPA = 17% of Qatar's LNG exports). Pearl GTL offline.
casualties_source: QatarEnergy / Reuters / Al Jazeera / Wood Mackenzie
notes: CRITICAL — World's largest LNG production facility handling ~20% of global LNG supply. IRGC struck in two waves (March 18-19) in retaliation for South Pars strike. QatarEnergy confirmed LNG Trains 4 and 6 damaged — 12.8 MTPA, representing 17% of Qatar's LNG exports. Repair timeline: 3-5 years. Force majeure declared on contracts to Belgium, Italy, South Korea, China. Estimated revenue loss: ~$20B/year (~9% of Qatar's GDP). Pearl GTL (Shell-operated) also damaged — offline minimum 1 year. Qatar expelled Iran's military and security attachés within 24 hours. Wood Mackenzie: 'fundamentally reshaping the global LNG outlook.' TTF gas surged ~30-33%.

casualties_reported: Minimal impact per Reuters. Ballistic missile aimed at Yanbu port intercepted.
notes: STRATEGIC — Yanbu is now Saudi Arabia's only functional crude export terminal due to Hormuz closure. SAMREF refinery (400,000 bpd JV between Aramco and ExxonMobil) struck by drone Mar 19 — 'minimal impact.' Ballistic missile aimed at Yanbu port intercepted. Mar 20: Saudi intercepted missile over northern Al Jouf, shot down 10 drones in east and 1 in north. Saudi ramped Yanbu volume to ~5.9 mbd through East-West pipeline (from pre-war 1-1.5 mbd). Saudi shot down at least 47 drones including 38 within a three-hour window over eastern oil-rich region on Mar 21.

casualties_reported: No casualties reported. Precautionary shutdowns.
notes: IRGC drone attacks hit two of Kuwait's three refineries March 19-20: Mina al-Ahmadi (346,000 bpd, fire in one unit) and Mina Abdullah (454,000 bpd, fire in one unit). Kuwait Petroleum Corporation issued precautionary shutdowns. Kuwait also thwarted a Hezbollah-linked terror cell with 10 arrests. Mar 21: Mina al-Ahmadi refinery struck by two waves of Iranian drones, sparking fires. Iran also claimed destruction of Ali Al Salem Air Base facilities.

city: Haifa BAZAN Group Oil Refinery, Israel
casualties_reported: Damage described as 'not significant' by Israel's Energy Minister.
notes: Iranian missile struck Haifa BAZAN Group oil refinery on March 19 in direct retaliation for the South Pars gas field strike. Israel's Energy Minister called the damage 'not significant.' Part of Iran's retaliatory campaign against energy infrastructure across the region.

casualties_reported: Shrapnel damage photographed by Israeli police. No casualties reported.
notes: Day 22: Missile fragments fell in occupied East Jerusalem, 350 meters from the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound. Multiple salvos targeted Jerusalem. Part of nine Iranian salvos on March 21.

city: Diego Garcia, British Indian Ocean Territory
origin: Iran — ~2,500 miles (4,000 km) from Iranian territory
casualties_reported: No impact on base. One missile failed in flight; US warship fired SM-3 interceptor at the other.
casualties_source: Wall Street Journal / IDF Chief of Staff Zamir
notes: UNPRECEDENTED RANGE — Iran fired two intermediate-range ballistic missiles at joint US-UK base on Diego Garcia, approximately 2,500 miles (4,000 km) from Iranian territory. Far exceeds Iran's previously acknowledged 2,000 km missile range. IDF Chief Zamir noted 4,000 km range puts European capitals (Berlin, Paris, Rome) within Iranian reach. Speculation centers on possible use of space launch vehicle to extend range. Neither struck the base.

origin: Iran (IRGC — part of '70th wave of attacks')
casualties_reported: 47 people hospitalized including 12-year-old boy in serious condition. Interceptors failed to knock down the missile.
notes: Iranian ballistic missile struck Dimona — city hosting Israel's Shimon Peres Negev Nuclear Research Center. IDF confirmed interceptors FAILED to knock down the missile. 47 hospitalized. IRGC called it 'revenge for Natanz.' Part of nine separate missile salvos from Iran on March 21.

city: Rishon Lezion / Bnei Brak / Shoham, Israel
casualties_reported: Building was empty — no casualties from daycare impact. 7 impact sites across area. Residential damage in Bnei Brak, Shoham, Rosh Ha'ayin.
notes: Iranian cluster bombs hit a daycare/kindergarten in central Israel. Building was empty. 7 impact sites recorded across the area including damage to residential buildings in Bnei Brak, Shoham, and Rosh Ha'ayin. Part of nine separate salvos on March 21.

casualties_reported: 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

weapon: Ballistic missiles with conventional warheads (hundreds of kg of explosives)
casualties_reported: 180-200 injured. Dimona: ~47 injured incl. 12-year-old boy (serious). Arad: ~84-116 injured incl. 5-year-old girl (serious), 10 serious, 19 moderate. Mass casualty event declared at Soroka Hospital, Beersheba. 8 killed per Greek City Times ONLY — UNVERIFIED.
casualties_source: Times of Israel / Al Jazeera / Haaretz / Jerusalem Post / PBS/AP / CNN
notes: At least 2 missiles penetrated Israeli air defenses and struck near Shimon Peres Negev Nuclear Research Center. 10+ apartment buildings damaged in Arad, 3 in danger of collapse. Near-miss on Dimona nuclear research center. No abnormal radiation detected (IAEA). Retaliation for US bunker-buster strike on Natanz same day. Iran's Ghalibaf: 'If the Israeli regime is unable to intercept missiles in the heavily protected Dimona area, it is operationally a sign of entering a new phase of the battle.'

city: Diego Garcia, British Indian Ocean Territory
weapon: 2 intermediate-range ballistic missiles (two-stage, ~4,000 km range; possibly improvised Simorgh space launch rocket)
casualties_reported: No casualties. Both missiles missed — 1 malfunctioned in flight, 1 intercepted (Euronews citing British official).
casualties_source: Bloomberg / CNN / Times of Israel / Euronews / CNBC / Fox News / WSJ
notes: First attack on Diego Garcia in history. Demonstrates Iranian missile range of ~4,000 km — double the previously self-imposed 2,000 km limit. IDF Chief Zamir: 'Europe is within range.' Raised alarm across European capitals. US-UK military base used for B-2 bomber staging and Tomahawk launches. UK approved US use of Diego Garcia and RAF Fairford for 'specific and limited defensive operations' against Iranian missile sites. UK MoD confirmed attack.

casualties_reported: Unknown — rocket trails photographed over city
notes: Day 24 (Mar 23): Fresh Iranian missile barrage struck over the Israeli coastal city of Netanya on Monday. Rocket trails photographed. Iranian strikes continued against Israel throughout the day despite Trump's announcement of 5-day power plant pause and claims of 'productive conversations.'

casualties_reported: Intercepted. Saudi expelled Iran's military attaché and 4 embassy staff within 24 hours.
notes: Day 23-24 (Mar 22-23): Saudi Arabia intercepted 3 ballistic missiles aimed at Riyadh plus multiple drones. In response, Saudi Arabia expelled Iran's military attaché and 4 embassy staff within 24 hours, citing 'flagrant violation' of international conventions.

casualties_reported: Cumulative: 6 US soldiers killed at Port Shuaiba, 2 Kuwaiti Navy servicemen killed, 67 army servicemen wounded.
notes: Day 23-24 (Mar 22-23): 9 ballistic missiles shot down over Kuwait in 24-hour period. Mina al-Ahmadi refinery (346,000 bpd) hit by drone March 20. Cumulative war toll: 6 US soldiers killed, 2 Kuwaiti Navy servicemen killed, 67 Kuwaiti army servicemen wounded.

casualties_reported: 7 killed (4 Qatari + 3 Turkish military)
notes: Day 24 (Mar 23): 7 killed in helicopter crash during routine duty — 4 Qatari and 3 Turkish military personnel. Separately, Ras Laffan LNG damage confirmed: 2 production trains knocked out — 12.8 MTPA lost (17% of Qatar's LNG exports), repair timeline 3-5 years, revenue loss ~$20B/year. QatarEnergy declared force majeure on entire LNG output.

casualties_reported: One person moderately-to-severely wounded
notes: Day 24 (Mar 23): Hezbollah launched missiles at northern Israel; one person in Kiryat Shmona moderately-to-severely wounded. IDF struck a Quds Force affiliate in Beirut in response. An Israeli civilian killed near the Lebanon border on March 22 may have been hit by Israeli artillery fire, not Hezbollah — IDF investigating.

casualties_reported: 6 wounded in Tel Aviv (MDA). 4 injured including 2 infants in southern Bedouin community.
casualties_source: CNN / Magen David Adom / Times of Israel
notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): Iran launched seven waves of ballistic missiles at Israel since midnight. At least 4 impact sites confirmed in central Tel Aviv — buildings damaged, vehicles burning. 6 people wounded per MDA. Sirens sounded in Dimona near Negev Nuclear Research Center. Cluster munition struck Nesher near Haifa with no injuries. 4 people including 2 infants injured when missile fragment hit a home in a southern Bedouin community.

casualties_reported: No new casualties reported.
notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): Kuwait air defense alarms sounded 7 times overnight during Iranian missile and drone attacks. Part of broader Iranian barrage across the Gulf.

casualties_reported: All intercepted. No casualties reported.
notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): Saudi Arabia intercepted approximately 20 drones targeting the Eastern Province oil region.

casualties_reported: 1 Indian national minor injuries from debris.
notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): UAE intercepted ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi. 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris.

casualties_reported: No casualties. Fuel tank hit, large fire. Damage limited to property.
notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Kuwait International Airport drone strike. Fuel tank hit, large fire. No casualties — 'damage limited to property.'

casualties_reported: All intercepted. No casualties reported.
notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Saudi Arabia intercepted 32+ drones and 1 ballistic missile over Eastern Province (near Ras Tanura, Ghawar, Abqaiq energy facilities) in 11 hours.

casualties_reported: No damage or injuries reported.
notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Iran targets Israel's Orot Rabin power plant in Hadera — largest energy facility providing 20-25% of electricity. No damage or injuries reported. Missile defense successful.

casualties_reported: 7 Iraqi soldiers killed, 13 wounded in Habbaniyah Military Clinic strike.
notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): US strike on Habbaniyah Military Clinic in Anbar Province killed 7 Iraqi soldiers and wounded 13. Iraq grants PMF 'right to respond' after Habbaniyah attack killed 15 PMF fighters (Mar 23). Iraq summons US and Iranian diplomats. Islamic Resistance in Iraq claims 23 operations in 24 hours.

casualties_reported: Impact sites in Kafr Qasim, Ganei Tikva, Kiryat Ata, and Tel Aviv. Iranian weapons capability 'down 90%+' per CENTCOM.
notes: Day 27 (Mar 26): Two salvoes of Iranian missiles targeted central and northern Israel within 30 minutes. Impact sites in Kafr Qasim, Ganei Tikva, Kiryat Ata, and Tel Aviv. CENTCOM stated Iranian weapons capability is 'down 90%+' compared to pre-war levels.

casualties_reported: 2 killed from intercepted missile debris.
notes: Day 27 (Mar 26): Two people killed in Abu Dhabi from intercepted missile debris. Total UAE casualties since war start: 9 killed (2 military, 7 civilian) across 357 ballistic missiles and 1,815 drone attacks.

casualties_reported: No casualties reported. Fire at facility.
notes: Day 27 (Mar 26): Fire reported at a facility in Bahrain following Iranian strikes.

casualties_reported: Intercepted. No casualties.
notes: Day 27 (Mar 26): Saudi Arabia intercepted a drone in its Eastern Province.

casualties_reported: 1 man in his 30s killed, 1 man in his 50s seriously injured.
notes: Day 27 (Mar 26): Hezbollah rocket strike on Nahariya killed a man in his 30s and seriously injured another in his 50s. Hezbollah chief Qassem rejected truce: 'negotiations under fire amounts to imposing surrender.' Israel expanding buffer zone toward Litani River.

city: Jerusalem / Netanya / Kfar Qasim / Ganei Tikva, Israel
weapon: Ballistic missiles with cluster warheads (7+ salvos)
casualties_reported: At least 10 wounded near Netanya, Kfar Qasim, and Ganei Tikva. Sirens in Jerusalem, West Bank, and central Israel.
notes: Day 28 (Mar 27): Iran fired at least 7 missile salvos at Israel within hours, many with cluster warheads. IRGC described it as 82nd wave of attacks. Missiles triggered sirens in Jerusalem, West Bank, and central Israel. IRGC drones also struck Haifa port.

casualties_reported: Unknown — port area struck
notes: Day 28 (Mar 27): IRGC drones struck Haifa port in what Iran described as its 82nd wave of attacks on Israel.

casualties_reported: Both drones shot down. No casualties.
notes: Day 28 (Mar 27): Kuwait's National Guard shot down two Iranian drones on March 27.

casualties_reported: 1 woman killed in Nahariya, 14 wounded. IDF Sgt. Aviaad Elchanan Volansky (21) killed by anti-tank fire in southern Lebanon.
casualties_source: IDF / Times of Israel / Al Jazeera
notes: Day 28 (Mar 27): Hezbollah fired 100+ rockets at Israel. A civilian woman killed in Nahariya. IDF Sgt. Volansky killed by anti-tank fire in southern Lebanon; funeral at Mount Herzl. IDF officer and soldier severely wounded by accidental grenade explosion. Israeli airstrikes hit Beirut's Burj al-Barajneh and Qlaileh. 162nd Division pushing toward Litani. Senior anti-tank commander Hassan Mohammad Bashir killed. Hezbollah chief Qassem vowed to fight 'without limits.'

casualties_reported: Israel intercepted at least one missile. No confirmed casualties.
notes: Day 29 (Mar 28): BREAKING — Houthis launched first military operation of the war — ballistic missile barrage targeting 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel. Sirens sounded in Beersheba near Dimona nuclear research center. Houthi spokesman Brig. Gen. Yahya Saree announced attack. Deputy information minister declared 'closing the Bab al-Mandeb strait is among our options.' ~30 tankers staged near Yanbu within Houthi strike range. Ends 28 days of Houthi restraint — opens third active front.

casualties_reported: 1 killed, 2 injured. 8 sites struck including a university.
notes: Day 29 (Mar 28): Iranian missiles struck 8 sites in Tel Aviv including a university. 1 killed, 2 injured. Iran has fired 500+ ballistic/naval missiles and ~2,000 drones since Day 1. Over 90% intercepted but attacks on radar systems have eroded the detection network. Missile launch rate declining — analysts cite stock depletion and rationing strategy.

city: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia
casualties_reported: At least 10 US troops injured. Refueling aircraft damaged.
notes: Day 29 (Mar 28): At least 10 US troops injured in Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia. Refueling aircraft damaged. CENTCOM reports 300+ total US wounded since Day 1.

casualties_reported: Airport radar damaged. No casualties reported.
notes: Day 29 (Mar 28): Kuwait International Airport radar damaged by Iranian drone strike.

city: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia
casualties_reported: 15+ US service members wounded (5 seriously). Refueling aircraft damaged.
notes: Day 29-30 (Mar 27-28 updated): Six ballistic missiles and 29 drones struck Prince Sultan Air Base, wounding 15+ US service members (5 seriously) and damaging refueling aircraft. Updated from initial report of 10 injured.

casualties_reported: Air raid sirens activated. Damage assessment pending.
notes: Day 30 (Mar 29): Air raid sirens activated in Bahrain and Kuwait as Iranian attacks continued. Part of sustained Iranian campaign against Gulf state targets.

casualties_reported: Intercepted — no impact confirmed
notes: Day 29-30 (Mar 28-29): Houthi missiles targeted 'sensitive Israeli military sites' in southern Israel (Beersheba/Dimona area). Intercepted. First Houthi attack on Israel since the 2026 war began.

casualties_reported: Damage assessment pending

casualties_reported: Intercepted. No casualties.

First Class Guy Ludar, 21, killed in Lebanon Apr 4, reportedly by friendly fire. Hezbollah claimed missile strike against Israeli warship off Lebanon's coast Apr 5; IDF said 'not aware' of incident. Israel ordered evacuation of Lebanon's Masnaa crossing with Syria. IDF expanding ground operations toward Litani River.

Houthi deputy information minister reiterated closing Bab al-Mandeb remains 'among our options.'

Video also showed interception of an Iranian missile over Jerusalem. Ceasefire stabilized by April 8.

casualties_reported: 2 civilians injured

Hezbollah halted attacks after the ceasefire announcement. However, Netanyahu explicitly stated the ceasefire does not apply to Lebanon — directly contradicting Pakistan's PM Sharif.

casualties_reported: Not confirmed — early reports of impacts in northern Israel
notes: Day 41 (Apr 9): In the early hours of April 9, Hezbollah launched retaliatory missile attacks on northern Israel in response to Israel's 'Operation Eternal Darkness' the previous day (Apr 8, 254 killed in Lebanon). Hezbollah lawmaker Hassan Fadlallah called the April 8 strikes 'a grave violation of the ceasefire.' The IDF admitted it overestimated damage to Hezbollah from the 2024 war — the group still has hundreds of launchers and tens of thousands of rockets, rebuilt with new weapons including FPV drones and possibly Russian anti-ship missiles. The escalation cycle on the Lebanon periphery threatens to pull the entire ceasefire down. Netanyahu's refusal to include Lebanon in the ceasefire is the core fracture. Soufan Center Apr 9 assessment: ceasefire 'hovers on the verge of collapse.'

casualties_reported: 1 IDF NCO severely wounded by suicide drone in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah rockets struck house in Kibbutz Misgav Am, school in Deir al-Asad, parking lot in Safed, caused fires in Kiryat Shmona. No civilian casualties confirmed.
notes: Days 42-43 (Apr 10-11): Hezbollah fired 30+ rockets at northern Israel on April 10 alone — striking Kibbutz Misgav Am (house hit), Deir al-Asad (school), Safed (parking lot), Kiryat Shmona (fires). Hezbollah also claimed attack on Ashdod naval base. Since the ceasefire began Apr 7, Hezbollah has fired 100+ rockets at Israel. One IDF NCO severely wounded by suicide drone in southern Lebanon. Cumulative Lebanon toll since March 2: 1,888-1,953 killed, 6,303+ wounded. Israel struck 120 Hezbollah sites in 24 hours around Apr 10. 14 killed in Lebanon Apr 10 including 13 Lebanese State Security personnel hit in government building in Nabatieh. Houthi activity limited but persistent: 4 attacks on Israel since resuming Mar 28 (all intercepted); no Red Sea shipping attacks resumed. Houthi leader declared ceasefire "a great victory."

casualties_reported: Apr 15: 5 IDF soldiers wounded (1 serious, 4 light) by Hezbollah rocket fire into northern Israel; woman wounded in Nahariya Apr 13. Apr 16: missile fired at Ashdod (Hezbollah claimed targeting of Ashdod Naval Base); missile damaged a 1,500-year-old church in Nahariya. No new Israeli fatalities confirmed in the Apr 15-16 window.
casualties_source: IDF / Hezbollah media / Alma Center / AP / Reuters
notes: Days 47-48 (Apr 15-16): Hezbollah launched ~30 rockets at northern Israel Apr 15 morning (IDF confirmed); claimed 24 separate attacks Apr 14 on northern Israel + IDF positions in southern Lebanon; shot down Israeli Hermes 450 drone and fired SAMs at Israeli warplanes. Apr 16: Hezbollah struck Ashdod (claimed Ashdod Naval Base) and damaged a 1,500-year-old church in Nahariya. Cumulative Hezbollah rocket/UAV activity since March 2: 1,121 attack waves (Alma Center). Hezbollah SecGen Naim Qassem rejected the Apr 14 Washington Israel-Lebanon talks as 'futile' and a 'free concession.' Hezbollah MP Hussein Hajj Hassan condemned the talks as 'a grave sin.' IDF Home Front Command warned Hezbollah may temporarily increase attacks to influence Washington negotiations. No Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks in the 48-hour window; Iraqi PMF quiet. IRGC Maj. Gen. Ali Abdollahi (Khatam al-Anbiya HQ) declared Apr 15 on Iranian state TV that Iran will 'not allow any exports or imports to continue in the Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, and the Red Sea' if US blockade continues — read by analysts (FDD/LWJ, WSJ) as potential Houthi activation signal. Mojtaba Khamenei 'military adviser' warned Iran would 'sink American ships in the Strait of Hormuz.' No kinetic Iranian action in the window.

casualties_reported: Pre-dawn Apr 16 Hezbollah rocket barrage on Kiryat Shmona started fires but caused no casualties. Hezbollah counter-claimed 39 operations including a D9 bulldozer hit by suicide drone. No confirmed Israeli fatalities in the final 24 hours pre-truce.
casualties_source: IDF / Hezbollah media / Al Jazeera / Reuters
notes: Day 49 (Apr 16-17): Final 24 hours before the Lebanon 10-day ceasefire that took effect 5:00 PM ET Apr 16 saw the war's most intense activity. IDF struck 380+ Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon in the day before the ceasefire, including an airstrike on Nabatieh and this pre-dawn Hezbollah rocket barrage on Kiryat Shmona (fires, no casualties). Battle of Bint Jbeil consolidation: Brig. Gen. Guy Levy's 98th Division Egoz Unit commandos raided a Hezbollah 'combat compound' in the Aynata casbah, seizing RPGs, Kalashnikovs, grenades, an anti-aircraft missile, and surveillance gear, then demolished ~70 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in under a minute via coordinated detonation. IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags. Hezbollah counter-claimed 39 operations including a D9 bulldozer hit by suicide drone. Cumulative Bint Jbeil toll: 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed. Lebanon ceasefire MoU 6-point: extension conditional on Lebanon 'effectively demonstrating its ability to assert its sovereignty'; Israel retains self-defense rights against 'planned, imminent, or ongoing attacks'; IDF troops remain south of the Litani River. IDF spokesman Avichay Adraee warned displaced Lebanese not to return south of the river yet; tens of thousands began moving southward Apr 17 despite warning. Hezbollah is NOT a signatory and declared its 'finger on the trigger'; DM Katz said the Lebanon campaign is 'not yet completed.' Iran front remains in ceasefire (~240 hrs). No new Iranian kinetic retaliation Apr 16-17. No Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks Apr 16-17. Wilson Center timeline through Apr 14: tempo declining — 1 ASBM intercepted Apr 11, ASBM into Gulf of Aden Apr 13, 4 drones destroyed Apr 14. Senior US military official told HASC Apr 16 Iran-backed Shiite militias have conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28; Iran retains 'thousands of missiles' and one-way attack drones.

city: Strait of Hormuz (Larak Island corridor / Gulf of Oman)
casualties_reported: No fatalities reported. Two IRGC gunboats opened fire on Indian-flagged VLCC Sanmar Herald (carrying Iraqi crude) despite prior clearance — ship continued via Iran-sanctioned Larak Island route. Minerva Evropi and Nissos Keros (both Indian-flagged) reversed course after taking fire attempting the neutral transit corridor. A container ship reported rocket damage from an unidentified projectile off the Omani coast (UKMTO advisory).
casualties_source: UKMTO / Kpler / TankerTrackers / WSJ / OSINTtechnical / Bloomberg
notes: Day 50 (Apr 18): Iran reimposed restrictions on the Strait of Hormuz within hours of Trump declaring 'no sticking points left' in a deal. At ~07:00-09:00 UTC Iran's Joint Military Command announced via IRIB that strait control had 'returned to its previous state ... under strict management and control of the armed forces,' accusing the US of 'piracy and maritime robbery' for maintaining its port blockade. Iranian Navy radio traffic monitored by Bloomberg and OSINT trackers instructed merchant vessels that 'no ships are allowed to pass through.' Approximately 20 outbound tankers reversed course inside the strait, per Kpler/TankerTrackers data cited by WSJ. Parliament security committee spokesman Ebrahim Rezaei announced Iran is drafting legislation to replace the informal ~$2M-per-vessel toll with a statutory 'strait security' charge — US and Israeli military vessels barred entirely. National Security Commission head Ebrahim Azizi confirmed ships now require prior Iranian naval authorization. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei (first public message since his Feb 28 elevation) used the Iranian Army anniversary to declare the navy 'ready to inflict bitter defeats on its enemies.' Polymarket odds of Hormuz returning to normal by end of April collapsed from 64% to ~32% on the reclosure news.

weapon: Attack on UNIFIL patrol (attribution alleged Hezbollah)
casualties_reported: 1 French chief warrant officer (CWO) serving with UNIFIL killed; 3 French peacekeepers wounded. Separately: 1 Lebanese killed in Israeli strike on a motorcycle in the south. Earlier MoPH reporting documented one death in Kounine overnight Apr 17.
casualties_source: Élysée / French MoD / Lebanese MoPH / Reuters / AP
notes: Day 50 (Apr 18): First major breach of the 48-hour-old Lebanon 10-day ceasefire and the first allied fatality on the Lebanon front. President Macron posted that 'everything suggests that responsibility for this attack lies with Hezbollah.' Netanyahu declared the anti-Hezbollah campaign 'not over' but Trump publicly told Israel it is 'prohibited' from striking Lebanon — 'enough is enough.' Lebanese President Aoun declared Lebanon will no longer be 'an arena for others' wars.' Lebanon MoPH end-Apr 17 cumulative: 2,294 killed, 7,544 wounded, ≥172 children, 100+ paramedics/health workers dead. Apr 8 'Black Wednesday / Operation Eternal Darkness' alone killed 357 and wounded 1,150+. Hezbollah losses: internal sources 400+ fighters, IDF claims 1,400+. The event will test whether Paris's diplomatic activism survives pressure to retaliate, particularly after the Apr 17 Paris summit where Macron co-hosted 49 countries (US excluded) for the Strait of Hormuz Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative.

city: Southern Lebanon / Bekaa Valley / southern Beirut
weapon: IDF airstrikes (vs Hezbollah targets); Hezbollah rocket/UAV fire
casualties_reported: ≥35 killed in Lebanon on Sunday Apr 19 — including a Red Cross paramedic — during the 10-day Lebanon truce. Haaretz Apr 18: Netanyahu privately admitted he has 'not succeeded' against Hezbollah. IDF reportedly struck 200+ Hezbollah targets over weekend Apr 18-19 (UNVERIFIED count). Cumulative Lebanon destruction: 40,000+ homes destroyed; 1,400+ buildings in south. Lebanon MoPH mid-April: 2,020-2,196 killed, 6,500-7,185 wounded; 177 children killed; 1.2 million displaced.
casualties_source: Lebanese authorities / Haaretz / Al Jazeera / Reuters / AP
notes: Day 52 (Apr 19-20): 10-day Israel-Lebanon truce (Apr 16-17 start; expires ~Apr 26-27) is holding 'shakily.' IDF maintains 'Operation Silver Plow' positions along a 10-km 'yellow line' buffer zone into southern Lebanon. Netanyahu (Apr 19 video to troops, flak jacket): 'The war continues, including within the security zone in Lebanon.' Battle of Bint Jbeil (ongoing since Apr 9): IDF 98th Division claims 100+ Hezbollah killed, town still contested. Apr 18 French UNIFIL Sgt 1st Class Florian Montorio killed in ambush (Macron blamed Hezbollah; denied). Lebanon-Israel diplomatic track: Ambassadors Nada Moawad and Yechiel Leiter preparing second State Dept meeting; Leiter publicly said 'next talks will be in a few weeks in Washington.' This entry captures the continued kinetic tempo during the ceasefire.

city: Strait of Hormuz / Gulf of Oman (~15nm NE of Oman + ~8nm off Iranian coast)
weapon: IRGC naval small-arms / fast-attack boats / boarding party
casualties_reported: No fatalities reported. ~03:38 ET / ~05:47 local UKMTO reported a container ship fired upon without radio warning ~15nm NE of Oman — 'heavy damage to the bridge,' crew safe. Second vessel struck ~8nm off Iranian coast shortly after. IRGC Navy confirmed SEIZURE of two container ships — Panama-flagged MSC Francesca and Greek-operated Epaminondas — escorting them to Iranian shores, claiming navigational-system tampering. Both vessels had AIS off at interception (CBS News). UPDATE (Apr 23-24): Greek Shipping Ministry (via vessel manager Technomar) CONFIRMED Epaminondas seizure (initially denied). Vessel Euphoria was DAMAGED but resumed sailing (not seized). Both ships were destined for India — escalating diplomatic incident with New Delhi; Panama's Foreign Ministry condemned seizure as 'grave attack on maritime security.' Vessels MSC Francesca and Epaminondas remain in Iranian waters, AIS-stationary ~10nm from Iranian coast.
casualties_source: UKMTO / CBS News / Reuters / Tasnim / Fars News / CNN
notes: Day 54 (Apr 22): The sharpest maritime escalation since the blockade began — IRGC opened fire on three commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz and seized two of them hours after Trump extended the Iran ceasefire indefinitely. Iran statement: 'Disrupting the order and safety of the Strait of Hormuz is our red line.' Follows Apr 20 USS Spruance (DDG-111) interdiction of Iranian-flagged M/V Touska in the Arabian Sea (Marines from 31st MEU rappelled from USS Tripoli helicopters and took the ship). FM Araghchi Apr 21 called the Touska seizure and broader blockade 'an act of war and a material breach of the ceasefire'; UN Amb. Iravani filed a formal protest letter with SecGen Guterres. CENTCOM tally (NPR Apr 21): 28 commercial vessels turned back since Apr 13 (up from 27 Apr 20). No UNSC resolution adopted Apr 21-22; Russia and China previously vetoed the Bahrain-drafted Hormuz resolution on Apr 7. Trump's indefinite ceasefire extension (~16:22 ET Apr 21 Truth Social) explicitly maintained the naval blockade; Islamabad Round 2 collapsed after Iran declined to attend. Ghalibaf adviser Mahdi Mohammadi called the extension 'a ploy to buy time for a surprise strike... continuation of the siege is no different from bombardment and must be met with a military response.' MSC and Greek operator both had AIS off at interception — a detail highlighted by CBS News analysts as evidence of anticipated maritime risk.

city: al-Tiri, southern Lebanon / Aynata-Bint Jbeil sector
weapon: IDF airstrikes / Commando Brigade direct action; Hezbollah denial
casualties_reported: Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil of Al-Akhbar KILLED in Israeli strike on village of al-Tiri (PM Nawaf Salam: 'war crime'; UN and CPJ condemn); 4-5 total killed in southern Lebanon Apr 22-23 incl. 5 killed Apr 22 and 4 more Apr 23 per Lebanese state media. IDF Commando Brigade killed 2 armed Hezbollah operatives near Aynata (adjacent to Bint Jbeil) Apr 23.
casualties_source: Al Jazeera / CBS News / CNN / Times of Israel / CPJ / Lebanese MoPH / Al-Akhbar
notes: Day 55 (Apr 23): Lebanon-Israel ceasefire (effective 17:00 ET Apr 16) continues to fray despite ongoing Washington diplomacy. Lebanese PM Salam called the killing of journalist Amal Khalil a 'war crime.' Channel 12 reports the assessment in Jerusalem 'is increasingly pointing to a resumption of fighting with Iran drawing closer.' On the same day, the second round of direct Israel-Lebanon ambassadors' talks convened in Washington (moved from State Dept to the White House); represented by Amb. Yechiel Leiter (Israel) and Amb. Nada Hamadeh Moawad (Lebanon); US added Mike Huckabee, Secretary Rubio expected to join for part. Lebanese position: no deal without full Israeli withdrawal. Retired BGen Mark Kimmitt told Al Jazeera the talks had 'functional flaws.' Cumulative Lebanon toll per Al Jazeera Apr 23 tracker: 2,294 killed / ≥7,658 wounded as of Apr 21; internal Hezbollah sources admit >1,000 fighters killed, IDF claims 1,700 fighters killed.

casualties_reported: Vessel name and flag not publicly specified by Iranian authorities at time of report. Iran said it seized a ship 'suspected of collaborating with American military' after it allegedly 'committed violations, ignored warnings.' No UKMTO or CENTCOM independent confirmation located in this research pass. MEDIUM confidence — single-source via Iranian state media/Wikipedia aggregation.
casualties_source: Iranian state media via Wikipedia (2026 Iran war / 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis)
notes: Day 56 (Apr 24): Iran announced a fourth maritime interception in three days, claiming it seized a ship 'collaborating with American military' that 'committed violations, ignored warnings.' This follows the Apr 22 IRGC seizure of MSC Francesca and Epaminondas. As of this entry's filing, no independent UKMTO, CENTCOM, or shipping-industry confirmation has been located — vessel name, flag, and present location not specified in Iranian media reports. Manifest flags as MEDIUM confidence pending independent corroboration. Iran's claim coincides with Iranian FM Araghchi's evening arrival in Islamabad (Apr 24) and the announced Witkoff/Kushner trip to Pakistan Apr 25 for direct US-Iran talks 'intermediated by the Pakistanis.'

casualties_reported: Lebanese Health Ministry: 2 KILLED in Israeli air strike on the southern town of Touline. Several wounded in separate Israeli artillery attack on Yater.
casualties_source: Al Jazeera (Apr 24) / Lebanese Health Ministry / CNN
notes: Day 56 (Apr 24): Israel continued kinetic operations in southern Lebanon despite the 3-week ceasefire extension Trump announced Apr 23. Lebanese MoPH reported 2 killed in Israeli air strike on Touline; several wounded in Israeli artillery strike on Yater. Cumulative Lebanese toll Apr 24: 2,491 killed / 7,719 wounded since Mar 2 — +37 killed vs Apr 21. UN Human Rights Office (OHCHR Geneva, Apr 24) said it has documented 'patterns of attacks on civilians in populated areas and residential buildings in Lebanon and Israel that may amount to serious violations of international humanitarian law' — strongest UN legal posture of the war to date. Hezbollah lawmaker Ali Fayyad called the truce extension 'meaningless.'

city: Bint Jbeil / Yater / Kafra / Ramyah, southern Lebanon
weapon: Ground combat / IDF strikes / Hezbollah ATGM and drone shootdown
casualties_reported: IDF says it 'eliminated' 6 Hezbollah fighters in exchange of fire in Bint Jbeil. IDF also struck rocket launchers in Yater and Kafra. Hezbollah confirmed shootdown of 1 Israeli drone and announced an attack on an IDF APC in Ramyah 'in response to home demolitions.' Israeli casualties not specified by IDF.
casualties_source: Al Jazeera / Haaretz / CNN / IDF / Hezbollah statements
notes: Day 56 (Apr 24): One of the heaviest direct exchanges since the Lebanon ceasefire took effect Apr 16 / was extended Apr 23. Netanyahu (Haaretz Apr 24) said Israel maintains 'full freedom of action against any threat' and accused Hezbollah of 'trying to derail talks.' UN OHCHR statement (Apr 24) that attacks on civilians in Lebanon/Israel may amount to serious IHL violations is the strongest UN legal posture of the war. Israeli DefMin Katz (Apr 23): Israel 'awaiting a green light' from the U.S. to resume war and 'complete the elimination of the Khamenei dynasty' / 'return Iran to the dark ages.' Total Lebanese casualties since Mar 2: 2,491 killed / 7,719 wounded.

city: Yohmor al-Shaqeef, Nabatieh district, southern Lebanon
weapon: Israeli air strike(s) on a truck and a motorbike
casualties_reported: Lebanese Public Ministry of Health: 4 KILLED; wounded unspecified.
casualties_source: Lebanese MoPH via Times of Israel, CBS News
notes: Day 57 (Apr 25): Lebanese Health Ministry reported two Israeli strikes — on a truck and on a motorbike — in Yohmor al-Shaqeef (Nabatieh district) killed four people. No immediate IDF comment per Times of Israel; IDF has previously said it 'continues to act decisively' against ceasefire violations. Within the Apr 17 Lebanon truce that was extended 3 weeks Apr 23. CNN noted at least 9 killed in Lebanon since the Apr 17 ceasefire took effect, with Apr 25 additions raising that figure. Cumulative Lebanese MoPH toll Apr 24 baseline: 2,491 K / 7,719 W; running total adjusts to 2,495 K with Apr 25 additions (subject to further MoPH consolidation). Hezbollah lawmaker Ali Fayyad (Apr 24) called the truce extension 'meaningless'; 'every Israeli attack gives Hezbollah the right to retaliate.'

casualties_reported: No casualties reported.
notes: Day 57 (Apr 25): IDF said it intercepted a 'suspicious aerial target' in southern Lebanon. Outcome: target did not cross into Israeli territory; IDF claimed it threatened forces in the area.

city: Strait of Hormuz / Larak Island vicinity
weapon: Iranian shadow-fleet vessel (commercial supertanker, no kinetic action)
